misconceptions.title: Slope Misidentification in Inequalities
misconceptions.description: Students confuse the slope of an inequality after failing to convert it into slope-intercept form. They often treat the coefficients of x and y as the slope without solving for y first.
successIndicators: Use dashed lines for strict inequalities

misconceptions.title: Improper Fraction Handling in Equations
misconceptions.description: Students incorrectly combine fractions or fail to factor them out before combining terms. They either neglect to clear fractions or improperly apply operations, resulting in inaccurate equations.

misconceptions.title: Switching Variables with Wrong Signs
misconceptions.description: Students switch variables x and y in systems of equations and apply the wrong sign to them. They fail to maintain the integrity of each variable in the equations.
successIndicators: Correctly identify variables and their functions

misconceptions.title: Mistaking Inequality Type from Boundary Line Style
misconceptions.description: Students mistakenly interpret solid and dashed lines as inclusive or exclusive inequalities without considering the inequality symbol. They err in shading the proper solution region.
successIndicators: Match line style to inequality type
Use symbols to determine line style
Shade correct region for the inequality
